summary: Draft/open matching branch for many Zako-related and adjacent functions. The central work implements grZakoGenerator spawning/cleanup/config logic, with additional camera, fighter, item, stage/material, and header typing matches across 71 files. It is useful as a source of matching tactics and struct-layout clues, but the PR is dirty/not merge-ready: a collaborator noted the branch touches too many files for an ours-merge strategy and needs manual conflict resolution, and the decomp.dev status comment reported many new matches alongside much larger regressions.

key_files.role: Main Zako generator match work; implements grZakoGenerator_801CA43C, _801CA67C, _801CA8B4, _801CACB8, _801CAE04, and _801CAF08 with item/stage spawning and cleanup.
Replaces several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with typed Zako generator signatures.
Adds struct ZakoConfig and changes grZakoGenerator_Entry.x4 to HSD_GObj*.
Adds full Camera_8002C1A8 pause/free-camera input handling implementation.
Refines item variable layouts for Tincle, WhiteBea, Oldottosea, and Lugia to support matched item code.
Large enemy/item behavior match set for Old Kuri motion init, phys, anim, and collision callbacks.
Large White Bea behavior match set including facing/collision setup, sound triggers, and state transitions.
Adds Lugia motion physics/transitions, local attribute structs, and a __frsqrte-based sqrt helper.
Adds Popo/Nana SpecialAirHi enter and air-physics callbacks with local dat_attrs views.
Reworks capture-pulled high collision naming/body placement and adds dont_inline pragmas/stack tactics.
Implements ground color/material overlay functions and adds raw overlay command/layout helpers.

what_changed: Converted many `/// #...` placeholders into C implementations across 71 changed files, with 2219 added and 249 deleted lines in the captured PR metadata.

Implemented Zako generator allocation, configuration sampling, per-slot spawn scanning, item/stage object creation, object index bookkeeping at item user_data offset 0xDD8, delayed respawn/cleanup behavior, and global removal of relevant Zako items.
Added typed Zako headers: `struct ZakoConfig`, typed `HSD_GObj*` storage in `grZakoGenerator_Entry`, and concrete signatures for generator functions instead of `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS`.

Implemented `Camera_8002C1A8` as a typed pause-camera input routine using `CameraInputs`, button bitmasks, 0.125 analog deadzones, zoom/move/rotate values, pause slot cycling, and pause-eye offset/distance resets.

Added fighter matches for Popo/Nana air SpecialHi enter/physics variants, Yoshi SpecialHi/SpecialAirHi enter, common rebirth animation/camera logic, and Kirby copied-eat state corrections from `ftKb_MS_EatWait`/`EatFall` to `ftKb_MS_Eat`/`EatAir`.

Added or improved item motion callbacks for Climbers ice, Clink milk, Flipper, Game & Watch chef, Hitodeman, Kamex, Kirby item helper, Kyasarin, Lugia, Matadogas, Mewtwo shadow ball, Old Kuri, Old Ottosea, Patapata, Pikachu Thunder, Seak needle thrown, Tincle, and White Bea.
Added stage/ground matches in Castle, Heal, Onett, Pushon, and Material code; `Ground` gained a concrete `xC0` float field used by material overlay logic.
Updated numerous headers and includes to replace unknown prototypes, expose needed externs, and align typed call sites with matched implementations.
smart_moves: Used small local attribute/layout structs with explicit padding and offsets instead of guessing whole data files: examples include `ZakoConfig`, `ftPpAirHiEnterAttrs`, `ftPpAirHiPhysAttrs`, `itLugiaMotionPhysAttrs`, `itOldottoseaInitAttrs`, and `grMaterial_801C9604_raw`.
Replaced broad unknown prototypes with specific `void`, `bool`, `s32`, `HSD_GObj*`, `Item_GObj*`, and `Ground_GObj*` signatures, improving cross-file type pressure for future matches.

Matched engine behavior by leaning on existing helpers (`Item_80268E5C`, `it_8027CAD8`, `it_8026D62C`, `ftCommon_Fall`, `mpCollSetFacingDir`, `lb_8000B1CC`, `Camera_8002BAA8`) instead of open-coding subsystems.

Preserved object-lifetime invariants in the Zako generator: spawned objects get a generator slot index, slots are cleared to `NULL`, sentinel `-1` is used for inactive entries, and respawn timers are set from stage-entry metadata.
Recognized codegen-sensitive helpers in fighter code by adding `#pragma dont_inline on` around selected functions and preserving stack-shaping artifacts.
Added item-var struct fields only where functions actually used them, making the layout evidence searchable without overnaming the whole structure.
decomp_lessons: Wide matching branches are hard to merge after master moves. The human comment explicitly says an attempted `git merge -s ours` strategy would not work because the branch/master diff touches too many files and conflicts need manual resolution.

Do not treat decomp.dev's headline new-match count alone as merge readiness. This PR's bot comment reported 190 new matches but also 1637 broken matches and large matched/linked-code drops, consistent with a dirty divergent draft branch.
When matching item and stage functions, first stabilize item variable layouts and header prototypes; otherwise call sites remain polluted by casts and `UNK_*` signatures.

For functions that are mostly state-machine glue, exact callback names and suffixes matter: `_Anim`, `_Phys`, `_Coll`, `_Destroyed`, `_HitShield`, and `_Spawned` signatures document how tables call the code.
Ground/material work may require converting padding into concrete fields (`Ground.xC0`) when a matched function stores state there; keep offset comments and audit downstream users.

Some matching code intentionally keeps apparent redundancy, manual absolute-value code, stack locals, pointer casts, and re-fetches of `GET_FIGHTER`/`GET_ITEM`; these can be required for register allocation and stack layout.
naming_conventions: Unknown-address functions remain address-based, e.g. `grZakoGenerator_801CA8B4`, `it_802D1830`, and `fn_801CADBC`, rather than inventing semantic names without evidence.
Known callbacks follow subsystem and state suffixes: `itOldkuri_UnkMotion2_Anim`, `itOldkuri_UnkMotion2_Phys`, `itOldkuri_UnkMotion2_Coll`, `ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Phys`, and `ftCo_Rebirth_Cam`.
Temporary layout structs are named after the function or item context plus role, such as `itLugiaMotionPhysAttrs`, `itWhiteBeaAnim7Attrs`, `ftCoRebirthCamVars`, and `grOnett_801E3CE4_vars`.
Headers keep address comments beside prototypes, e.g. `/* 1CA8B4 */ s32 grZakoGenerator_801CA8B4(int);`.
Item variable structs in `itCommonItems.h` retain offset comments and `xNN` field names when semantics are not known.
File-local stage callback tables can be made `static` when the diff shows no external use, as with `grOt_803E27E0`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop` around small fighter helper functions to control inlining.
Used `PAD_STACK` or dummy `u8 _[...]` locals to force stack frame size and local ordering.
Kept a documented `FAKE MATCH` comma-operator expression in Kirby SpecialN code for register allocation.
Used manual absolute-value branches instead of library `fabsf` in several physics/input routines.
Used a local `it_802D1830_sqrt` based on `__frsqrte` refinement for Lugia rather than a generic sqrt call.
Shaped the camera stack with a padded local struct containing `CameraInputs` before reading input fields.
Used explicit pointer arithmetic/casts for raw layout matches, e.g. `(u8*) gp + 0x40`, `(u8*) spawned->user_data + 0xDD8`, and bitfield extraction in `grMaterial_801C9490_cmd`.
review_feedback: No formal review records or inline review comments were present in the slice.

Collaborator ribbanya wrote that an initially considered `git merge -s ours` approach would discard incoming master changes and was not viable because the branch/master diff touches too many different files; conflicts need manual resolution.
The PR metadata marks the PR as draft/open, mergeable false/dirty in the captured GitHub API data.

The decomp.dev bot comment for this PR reported 190 new matches but 1637 broken matches, with matched code down 11.05% and linked code down 7.00%, so the branch should be mined carefully rather than merged wholesale.
